{{infobox Shinto shrine |name = Sugiyama Shrine (杉山神社) |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}}{{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}}{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо}} 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674}})
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}} on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀}}): {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, {{ill|1920|ja|1920}} (9th year of the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674}}) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of {{ill|Awa Shrine|ja|安房神社}}, who belonged to the {{ill|Kibe clan|ja|忌部氏}} of the Kii region ({{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国|lt=Kishu|lt_ja=紀州}} lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities {{ill|Takami-musubi no Mikoto|ja|タカミムスビ|lt=Takami-musubi no Mikoto|lt_ja=高御座巣日太命}} (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient {{ill|Tokaido (region)|ja|東海道|lt=Tokaido|lt_ja=東海道}} served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the {{ill|Tsurumi River|ja|鶴見川}} {{ill|Water system|ja|水系}} ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭}}: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|lt=Fudō|lt_ja=不動}}. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社}}, which is located in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区 (横浜市)|lt=Aoba|lt_ja=青葉区}}{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from {{ill|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|ja|横浜市営地下鉄ブルーライン}} at {{ill|Center South Station|ja|センター南駅}} == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|sugiyama shinja yokohama tsuzuki kucakasi chuo}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Local Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]]

shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|12=draft}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient {{ill|Tokaido (region)|ja|東海道|lt=Tokaido|lt_ja=東海道|12=draft}} served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|12=draft}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|12=draft}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the {{ill|Tsurumi River|ja|鶴見川|12=draft}} {{ill|Water system|ja|水系|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|12=draft}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|12=draft}}: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft}} (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|lt=Fudō|lt_ja=不動|12=draft}}. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|12=draft}}, which is located in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区 (横浜市)|lt=Aoba|lt_ja=青葉区|12=draft}}{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|12=draft}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from {{ill|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|ja|横浜市営地下鉄ブルーライン|12=draft}} at {{ill|Center South Station|ja|センター南駅|12=draft}} == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=draft}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|12=draft}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=draft}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=draft}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|sugiyama shinja yokohama tsuzuki kucakasi chuo}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Local Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Mass Imported Shinto Cult Pages]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 杉山神社 |画像 = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[都筑区]][[茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|茅ケ崎中央]]57-18 |祭神 = [[五十猛命]] |社格 = 式内小社、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 伝・[[白鳳]]3年（[[674年]]）
※『麗気記私抄』等による。 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[10月13日]] |神事 = |緯度度=35|緯度分=32|緯度秒=55.41|N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度=139|経度分=34|経度秒=22.74|E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 =Yokohama |ラベル位置=bottom |}} '''杉山神社'''（すぎやまじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[都筑区]]にて、[[都筑中央公園]]東端の[[丘|高台]]に鎮座している[[神社]]。旧[[茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|茅ヶ崎村]]の[[郷社]]で[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu2 武蔵の五十猛命 神奈川県横浜市：茅ヶ崎　杉山神社]（神奈備）、横浜市内唯一かつ旧[[武蔵国]]の[[式内社]]である「[[杉山神社]]」の有力[[延喜式神名帳#論社|論社]]の一つとされている。 == 祭神 == * [[五十猛命]] * 配祀神（[[合祀]]）：[[天照大神]]、[[倉稲魂神]]、[[素戔嗚尊]][http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php 杉山神社 (茅ヶ崎中央)]（あかね台.net） なお、神奈川県神社庁のサイトでは天照大神が[[祭神]]となっている[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 杉山神社]（神奈川県神社庁）。また、後述のように当初は祭神が異なっていたことが記されている[[文献資料 (歴史学)|文献資料]]があり、さらに[[日本武尊]]が祀られていたという伝えも残っている。 == 由緒 == 境内の[[1920年]]（[[大正]]9年）10月13日建立「神社合祀記録碑」や『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』によると、[[天武天皇]][[白鳳]]3年（[[674年]]）、[[安房神社]]神主の忌部勝麻呂（[[紀伊国|紀州]]系[[忌部氏]]）によって武蔵国の杉山の岡に[[タカミムスビ|高御座巣日太命]]（高御産日命）・[[天日鷲神|天日和志命]]（天日鷲命）・[[阿八別彦命|由布津主命]]（阿八別彦命）の3柱（現在の祭神とは異なっている）が祀られたことが記されており{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿茅ヶ崎村杉山神社}}、同氏の麻穀栽培地開墾の拡大とともに神社も広まったとされる。当時の[[都筑郡]]は開拓者である忌部氏の勢力が強く、古[[東海道]]も横断し物資の集散地であった。ただし、杉山神社の始まりが忌部勝麻呂によるものとされることについては、他に文献資料などが残っておらず根拠としては乏しいとされる[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html 杉山神社巡り]（神のやしろを想う）[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php 杉山神社リスト]（あかね台.net）[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 杉山神社]（Tomのスペース 2011年10月30日）。 == 式内社の論社として == 『新編武蔵風土記稿』では「恐ラク当社コソ昔ノ式社ナルヘケレ」と記されており{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿茅ヶ崎村杉山神社}}、[[郷土史]]家の[[戸倉英太郎]]も『杉山神社考』の中で当社を式内社の「杉山神社」と[[延喜式神名帳#論社|比定]]している[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ 港北区の歴史と文化（シリーズ　わがまち港北 2003年4月号）：第52回 杉山神社の有力候補]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所 ）。なお当社も他の多くの論社と同じく、[[鶴見川]][[水系]]（[[早渕川]]）沿いの高台に鎮座している。 当社境内から背後の[[丘陵]]上にかけては、[[縄文時代]]前期の[[貝塚]]と、[[弥生時代|弥生]]・[[古墳時代]]集落の[[複合遺跡]]である[[境田貝塚]]が分布している{{Sfn|横浜市教育委員会生涯学習部文化財課|2004}}{{Cite web|和書|author=横浜市教育委員会|url=http://wwwm.city.yokohama.lg.jp/index.asp?dtp=9|title=横浜市行政地図情報提供システム文化財ハマSite|work=横浜市|accessdate=2022-05-16}}。 == 境内社 == * [[稲荷神社]] == 祭事・年中行事 == * [[例祭|例大祭]]：[[10月13日]]（秋祭り） == 御神体 == 吉田東悟『地名辞書』によると、当社の[[御神体]]は[[不動明王|不動]]の像が刻まれた円経10cmの[[銅鏡]]とされる。 == その他 == 当社には[[神職]]が常駐しておらず、[[青葉区 (横浜市)|青葉区]][[荏田町]]にある[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 劔神社]の兼務社となっている。 == 所在地・交通 == 神奈川県横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央57-18 * [[横浜市営地下鉄ブルーライン]]「[[センター南駅]]」より徒歩約6分 == ギャラリー == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|当社の社号標と一つ目の[[鳥居]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|一つ目の鳥居 File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|階段の途中にある二つ目の鳥居 File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|境内の稲荷神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ85都筑郡ノ5|chapter=茅ヶ崎村|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿茅ヶ崎村杉山神社}}}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=横浜市教育委員会生涯学習部文化財課|title=横浜市文化財地図|publisher=横浜市教育委員会|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * [[杉山神社]] - 横浜市を中心に数十社分布する。当社の他には以下の有力論社がある。 ** [[杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] - 武州六大明神の一つで、[[くらやみ祭]]（武蔵国国府祭）にも参列している。 ** [[杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)]] ** [[杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)]] - 当社の北東約700mに鎮座している。 * [[鶴見神社 (横浜市)]] *[[都筑中央公園]] *[[境田貝塚]] ==外部リンク== * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 杉山神社]（神奈川県神社庁） * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php 杉山神社 (茅ヶ崎中央)]（あかね台.net） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すきやましんしや よこはましつつきくちかさきちゆうおう}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:都筑区の建築物]] [[Category:都筑区の歴史]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:杉山神社|ちかさき]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] to [[Sugiyama Shrine (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

杉山神社巡り]（神のやしろを想う）[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php 杉山神社リスト]（あかね台.net）[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 杉山神社]（Tomのスペース 2011年10月30日）。 == 式内社の論社として == 『新編武蔵風土記稿』では「恐ラク当社コソ昔ノ式社ナルヘケレ」と記されており{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿茅ヶ崎村杉山神社}}、[[郷土史]]家の[[戸倉英太郎]]も『杉山神社考』の中で当社を式内社の「杉山神社」と[[延喜式神名帳#論社|比定]]している[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ 港北区の歴史と文化（シリーズ　わがまち港北 2003年4月号）：第52回 杉山神社の有力候補]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所 ）。なお当社も他の多くの論社と同じく、[[鶴見川]][[水系]]（[[早渕川]]）沿いの高台に鎮座している。 当社境内から背後の[[丘陵]]上にかけては、[[縄文時代]]前期の[[貝塚]]と、[[弥生時代|弥生]]・[[古墳時代]]集落の[[複合遺跡]]である[[境田貝塚]]が分布している{{Sfn|横浜市教育委員会生涯学習部文化財課|2004}}{{Cite web|和書|author=横浜市教育委員会|url=http://wwwm.city.yokohama.lg.jp/index.asp?dtp=9|title=横浜市行政地図情報提供システム文化財ハマSite|work=横浜市|accessdate=2022-05-16}}。 == 境内社 == * [[稲荷神社]] == 祭事・年中行事 == * [[例祭|例大祭]]：[[10月13日]]（秋祭り） == 御神体 == 吉田東悟『地名辞書』によると、当社の[[御神体]]は[[不動明王|不動]]の像が刻まれた円経10cmの[[銅鏡]]とされる。 == その他 == 当社には[[神職]]が常駐しておらず、[[青葉区 (横浜市)|青葉区]][[荏田町]]にある[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 劔神社]の兼務社となっている。 == 所在地・交通 == 神奈川県横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央57-18 * [[横浜市営地下鉄ブルーライン]]「[[センター南駅]]」より徒歩約6分 == ギャラリー == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|当社の社号標と一つ目の[[鳥居]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|一つ目の鳥居 File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|階段の途中にある二つ目の鳥居 File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|境内の稲荷神社 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ85都筑郡ノ5|chapter=茅ヶ崎村|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿茅ヶ崎村杉山神社}}}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=横浜市教育委員会生涯学習部文化財課|title=横浜市文化財地図|publisher=横浜市教育委員会|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * [[杉山神社]] - 横浜市を中心に数十社分布する。当社の他には以下の有力論社がある。 ** [[杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] - 武州六大明神の一つで、[[くらやみ祭]]（武蔵国国府祭）にも参列している。 ** [[杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)]] ** [[杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)]] - 当社の北東約700mに鎮座している。 * [[鶴見神社 (横浜市)]] *[[都筑中央公園]] *[[境田貝塚]] ==外部リンク== * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 杉山神社]（神奈川県神社庁） * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php 杉山神社 (茅ヶ崎中央)]（あかね台.net） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すきやましんしや よこはましつつきくちかさきちゆうおう}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:都筑区の建築物]] [[Category:都筑区の歴史]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:杉山神社|ちかさき]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name = Sugiyama Shrine (杉山神社) |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]]{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Chuo|qq=}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/674|qq=}})
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, {{ill|1920|ja|1920|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}} (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/674|qq=}}) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|version=103300784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]]

※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, {{ill|1920|ja|1920|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}} (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/674|qq=}}) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|version=103300784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name = Sugiyama Shrine (杉山神社) |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]]{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Chuo|qq=}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/674|qq=}})
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, {{ill|1920|ja|1920|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}} (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|version=103300784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:都筑区の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name = Sugiyama Shrine (杉山神社) |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]]{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Chuo|qq=}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/674|qq=}})
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, {{ill|1920|ja|1920|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}} (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/674|qq=}}) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|version=103300784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:都筑区の建築物]] [[Category:都筑区の歴史]]

Tokura|qq=}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|version=103300784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:都筑区の歴史]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name = Sugiyama Shrine (杉山神社) |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]]{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Chuo|qq=}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/674|qq=}})
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, {{ill|1920|ja|1920|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}} (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ({{ill|674|ja|674|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/674|qq=}}) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|version=103300784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki Ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama]]
