スサノオ}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, [[:en:1920|1920]] (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ([[:en:674|674]]) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine, Yokohama|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|version=103300784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama]] {{Commons category|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}}

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]]{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Chuo|qq=}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 3 ([[:en:674|674]])
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine, Yokohama|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) {{Shinto in Yokohama}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|version=103300784|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama]] {{Commons category|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}}

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tsuzuki-ku|qid=Q1153920}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.548725|N|139.57298333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Isotakeru|qid=Q10881556}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17994298]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama jinjya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name = Sugiyama Shrine (杉山神社) |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]]{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Chuo|qq=|1=Chigasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617146}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ([[:en:674|674]])
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=|1=Tsuzuki Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643779}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=|1=Chigasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617146}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, [[:en:1920|1920]] (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ([[:en:674|674]]) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=|1=Tsuzuki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643787}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=|1=Tokura Eitaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108857513}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=|1=Edachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618320}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama jinjya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521945}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama jinjya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994294}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama jinjya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994300}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi-jinja|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=|1=Tsuzuki Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643779}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=|1=Sakaida Shell Midden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112048073}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=|1=Chigasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617146}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, [[:en:1920|1920]] (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ([[:en:674|674]]) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=|1=Tsuzuki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643787}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=|1=Tokura Eitaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108857513}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=|1=Edachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618320}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521945}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994294}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994300}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=|1=Tsuzuki Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643779}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=|1=Sakaida Shell Midden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112048073}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) * full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama]] Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]]{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Chuo|qq=|1=Chigasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617146}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ([[:en:674|674]])
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=|1=Tsuzuki Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643779}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=|1=Chigasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617146}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} * Enshrined Deities on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=|1=Tokura Eitaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108857513}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=|1=Edachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618320}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521945}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994300}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tsuzuki-ku|qid=Q1153920}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.548725|N|139.57298333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Isotakeru|qid=Q10881556}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17994298]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama jinjya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name = Sugiyama Shrine (杉山神社) |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]]{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Chuo|qq=|1=Chigasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617146}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ([[:en:674|674]])
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=|1=Tsuzuki Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643779}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=|1=Chigasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617146}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, [[:en:1920|1920]] (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ([[:en:674|674]]) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=|1=Tsuzuki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643787}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=|1=Tokura Eitaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108857513}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tsuzuki-ku|qid=Q1153920}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.548725|N|139.57298333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Isotakeru|qid=Q10881556}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17994298]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama jinjya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name = Sugiyama Shrine (杉山神社) |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 04.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|55.4|N|139|34|22.7|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]]{{ill|Chigasaki Chuo|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Central|lt_ja=茅ケ崎中央|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Chuo|qq=|1=Chigasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617146}}57-18 |deity = {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Shikinaisha (small shrine), Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally, in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ([[:en:674|674]])
※ According to sources such as “Reikeki Shishō”. |main_building = |alias = |festival = [[:en:October 13|October 13]] |rituals = |latitude_degrees = 35|latitude_minutes = 32|latitude_seconds = 55.41|N (North Latitude/South Latitude) = N |longitude_degrees = 139|longitude_minutes = 34|longitude_seconds = 22.74|E (East Longitude/West Longitude) = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Yokohama |label_position = bottom }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] on a hill at the eastern end of {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=|1=Tsuzuki Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643779}}. It is a former local shrine of {{ill|Chigasaki Village|ja|茅ケ崎 (横浜市)|lt=Chigasaki Village|lt_ja=茅ヶ崎村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Village|qq=|1=Chigasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617146}} and is considered one of the prominent candidate shrines ({{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ren-sha|lt_ja=論社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}) of the only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, [[:en:1920|1920]] (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ([[:en:674|674]]) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=|1=Tsuzuki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643787}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=|1=Tokura Eitaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108857513}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=|1=Edachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618320}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521945}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994294}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994300}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=|1=Tsuzuki Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643779}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=|1=Sakaida Shell Midden|comment=changed title based on only Shikinaisha in Yokohama City from the former [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Itsutake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} * Enshrined Deities ({{ill|Gōshi|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōshi|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}): [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Kura Inatama no Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inatama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_14.php Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki Chuo)] (Akanedai.net) Note: On the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency website, Amaterasu is listed as the deity.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) Additionally, there are historical documents indicating that the original deity was different and that {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} was once enshrined. == History == According to the “Shrine Joint Enshrinement Record Stone” established on October 13, [[:en:1920|1920]] (9th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) within the precincts, and the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, the shrine was enshrined in Hakuhō 3 ([[:en:674|674]]) by Kibe Katsumaro, the priest of [[:en:Awa Shrine|Awa Shrine]], who belonged to the [[:en:Inbe clan|Kibe clan]] of the Kii region ([[:en:Kii Province|Kishu]] lineage), at Sugiyama Hill in Musashi Province, with the three deities [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takami-musubi no Mikoto]] (also known as Takami-musubi no Mikoto), {{ill|Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|lt_ja=天日和志命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amateruhiwashi no Mikoto), and {{ill|Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|阿八別彦命|lt=Yufutsunushi no Mikoto|lt_ja=由布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ababetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Yufutsunushi no Mikoto)—which differ from the current deity.{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} The expansion of Kibe Katsumaro's millet cultivation lands is said to have contributed to the spread of the shrine. At that time, {{ill|Tsuzuki District|ja|都筑郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki District|qq=|1=Tsuzuki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643787}} was dominated by the pioneering Kibe clan, and the ancient [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tokaido]] served as a distribution hub for goods. However, due to a lack of additional documentary evidence, the claim that Sugiyama Shrine originated with Kibe Katsumaro is considered to have insufficient basis.[http://skyimpulse.s26.xrea.com/sugiyama.html Sugiyama Shrine Tour] (In Memory of Kami Yashiro)[http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama.php Sugiyama Shrine List] (Akanedai.net)[http://tytsmed.wordpress.com/2011/10/30/%E6%9D%89%E5%B1%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sugiyama Shrine] (Tom's Space, October 30, 2011) == As one of the Candidate Shrines of the Shikinaisha == In the “{{ill|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}”, it is stated, "Surely our shrine must have once been a Shikinaisha,"{{sfn|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}} and local historian {{ill|Eitaro Tokura|ja|戸倉英太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eitaro Tokura|qq=|1=Tokura Eitaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108857513}}, in his work “Consideration on Sugiyama Shrine”, also identified this shrine as the Shikinaisha Sugiyama Shrine {{ill|Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Identified|lt_ja=比定|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Jinmyōchō|qq=|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): Sugiyama Shrine as a prominent candidate] (Okura Mental Culture Research Institute) Moreover, like many other candidate shrines, this shrine is located on a hill along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|Water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|qq=|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例大祭|lt=Reidai Festival|lt_ja=例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] (Autumn Festival) == Sacred Objects == According to Higashisato Yoshida's "Gazetteer," the sacred object of the shrine is a 10 cm bronze mirror inscribed with the image of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]]. == Other == The shrine does not have resident priests; instead, it is concurrently administered by {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}}, which is located in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Yokohama|Aoba]]{{ill|Eda-machi|ja|荏田町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eda-machi|qq=|1=Edachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618320}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=203&cd=1202108&scd=000&npg=0 Tsurugi Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) == Location and Transportation == 57-18, Chigasaki Chuo, Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture * Approximately a 6‑minute walk from [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line]] at [[:en:Center-Minami Station|Center South Station]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 01.jpg|The shrine's nameplate and the first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 02.jpg|The first [[:en:Torii|Torii]] File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 03.jpg|The second [[:en:Torii|Torii]] located midway up the stairs File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo) 05.jpg|The [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 85, Tsuzuki District No.5|chapter=Chigasaki Village|publisher=Geographical Department, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/68}}|ref={{sfnref|New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki Chigasaki Village Sugiyama Shrine}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author=Cultural Property Division, Yokohama City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Department|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|publisher=Yokohama City Board of Education|year=2004|date=2004-03|ncid=BB23262051|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly in Yokohama City. In addition to this shrine, there are the following prominent candidate shrines: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Midori-ku, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521945}} – One of the six major shrines of Musashi, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Kokufu Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Kohoku Ward, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994294}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Tsuzuki Ward, Yokohama)|qq=|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994300}} – Located approximately 700 m to the northeast of this shrine. * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} * {{ill|Tsuzuki Chuo Park|ja|都筑中央公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Chuo Park|qq=|1=Tsuzuki Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643779}} * {{ill|Sakaida Shell Midden|ja|境田貝塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaida Shell Midden|qq=|1=Sakaida Shell Midden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112048073}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=170&cd=1202075&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *
